# JST

> JavaScript Templates (JST) : reactive web components in plain HTML, with
> JavaScript itself as the templating language. No build step. JST is vendored
> into apps as plain files (never an npm install), and releases are git tags.

If you are an AI agent working on a page or app that uses JST, read the JST
skill first. It is the canonical agent-facing instruction set: component
authoring, jst-nav, the token/theming contract, and the known gotchas.

## Agent instructions

- [JST skill (latest)](https://br3nt.github.io/jst/.claude/skills/jst/SKILL.md):
  matches the current main branch.
- Version-pinned skill: the app you are in carries its own vendored copy of
  JST. Find its version with `grep "export const version" jst.js`, then fetch
  the skill that matches it:
  `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/br3nt/jst/v<version>/.claude/skills/jst/SKILL.md`

## Server interactions: the HATEOAS way

Aimed at coding agents that default to JSON. When you build an interaction on a
JST page, the reflex to reach for a JSON endpoint plus a client-side renderer is
almost always wrong. The HATEOAS way: a UI state transition goes out over a form
or a link (form-encoded body or query string), and the server answers with a
rendered HTML fragment that replaces the stale region. There is no client model
to keep in sync — the DOM is the state.

Rules to follow:

- A state transition is a form submit or a link, never a hand-built `fetch` of a
  JSON API. Programmatic causes (a keystroke, a drop, a poll) call
  `swap(target, url, { method, body })` with a `URLSearchParams` body — still
  form-encoded, still HTML back.
- Form-encoded in, rendered HTML fragment out. Never design a JSON endpoint for
  something a rendered fragment can express.
- Use `jst-swap="morph"` for stateful regions (a list, a board, a field) so
  focus, scroll and open/closed state survive; key children with `jst-key`.
- When one transition changes several regions, respond with the **smallest
  common ancestor** fragment that contains them all, and morph it — not several
  out-of-band pieces.
- Affordances travel inside the returned HTML: an undo action is a form in the
  response, the next poll is a `data-poll` URL (or `onreveal`) on the returned
  fragment, a validation message is the field re-rendered. When the server stops
  emitting the trigger, the behaviour stops — no client bookkeeping.
- Escape any user-derived text you interpolate into a fragment.

The ten worked recipes (live demos, a visible wire log proving no JSON crosses
the wire, and a plain Rails `render partial:` sketch each) are at
[examples/hateoas_recipes.html](https://br3nt.github.io/jst/examples/hateoas_recipes.html):

1. Drag-and-drop list reorder — drop POSTs the move; server returns the whole re-rendered list; morph.
2. Kanban card move — drop POSTs card+column; server returns the board fragment (smallest common ancestor); morph.
3. Click to edit — GET swaps in the edit form; POST returns the display fragment, or the form re-rendered with an error.
4. Toggle with dependent counter — POST flips state; the fragment re-renders the control and the "N starred" counter together.
5. Server-side validation on blur — blur POSTs the field; server returns it re-rendered valid/invalid; the rule lives once.
6. Bulk actions — one form of checkboxes POSTs every checked id; server returns the re-rendered table + toolbar.
7. Delete with undo — the response carries the updated list and a toast whose form describes the inverse action.
8. Autosave — debounced POST of the draft; response is a tiny rendered status fragment, not a silent 204.
9. Long job with progress — progress fragment carries its own re-poll trigger; the finished fragment omits it, so polling stops.
10. Search as you type — GET with the query in the URL, rendered `<li>`/`<option>` back (cross-links the jst-nav and combobox demos).

## Docs

- [README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/br3nt/jst/main/README.md): what
  JST is, the philosophy, the component API in brief.
- [Onboarding walkthrough](https://br3nt.github.io/jst/docs/): one page,
  ordered to teach, annotated live examples.
- [index.d.ts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/br3nt/jst/main/index.d.ts):
  authoritative core API surface.
- [jst-nav.d.ts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/br3nt/jst/main/jst-nav.d.ts):
  authoritative navigation API surface.
- [CHANGELOG](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/br3nt/jst/main/CHANGELOG.md):
  semantics changes release by release.
